# Joshua Opolko

> Joshua Opolko's personal site: hands-on self-hosting guides (n8n, SearXNG, Dify, CrewAI), first-hand AI-tooling comparisons, and essays on technology, psychology, and society.

## Articles

- [Self-Hosting n8n for LangChain AI Workflows: A Deployment Evaluation](https://joshuaopolko.com/n8n-langchain-self-hosting/) (2026-07-04): Evaluating self-hosted n8n as a company LangChain platform: how the AI Agent nodes actually use LangChain, licensing, queue-mode scaling, resource sizing, data privacy, and when Flowise or Langflow fit better.
- [Reverse Terraforming: How the Space Industry Is Remodelling the Atmosphere](https://joshuaopolko.com/reverse-terraforming/) (2026-07-02): The space industry calls burning up on re-entry environmentally neutral. It isn't. Vaporising thousands of satellites injects alumina nanoparticles into the stratosphere where they persist for years, catalyse ozone destruction, and alter the planet's radiation budget. Nobody owns the stratosphere, and nobody is cleaning it up.
- [VR Hardware Sizing for a 30-Person Architecture Firm: Workstations, GPUs, Servers](https://joshuaopolko.com/vr-hardware-sizing-aec/) (2026-07-02): Exactly how many workstations, GPUs, servers, and headsets a 30-person AEC firm needs for VR rendering, with worked bills of materials for the three deployment models and scaling rules for 10 and 50 seats.
- [Scaling VR Visualization in Growing AEC Teams: A Practical Playbook](https://joshuaopolko.com/vr-visualization-growing-teams/) (2026-07-02): How VR visualization practice changes as an architecture or construction team grows from one champion to a fleet: toolchain standardization, headset fleets, skills, and review process by team size.
- [VR Walkthrough Solutions for AEC Firms: Tools, Delivery, Client Sessions](https://joshuaopolko.com/vr-walkthrough-aec/) (2026-07-02): How AEC firms deliver VR walkthroughs in 2026: tethered PCVR, standalone headset streaming, and no-install WebXR, plus a field-tested protocol for running client sessions that produce decisions.
- [Qwen2.5-VL: The Local Vision Model That Reads License Plates, Serial Numbers, and Street Signs](https://joshuaopolko.com/qwen2-5-vl/) (2026-06-27): Qwen2.5-VL reads real-world text from images through pure visual inference, no external database. Run the 7B model on a gaming GPU. OCRBench 864 vs GPT-4o-mini 785. First-person results.
- [Walkable 1:1 Scale Pavilion: Architectural Daylighting Demo in Three.js WebXR](https://joshuaopolko.com/walkable-1-1-scale-room-three-js-webxr-architecture-demo/) (2026-06-24): Walk through a concrete-and-glass pavilion at true 1:1 scale. Drag the sun slider to sweep shadows across the polished concrete floor. Three.js WebXR, no install.
- [n8n vs Windmill: Which Self-Hosted Workflow Engine to Run](https://joshuaopolko.com/n8n-vs-windmill/) (2026-06-20): n8n vs Windmill for self-hosting: low-code visual automation versus code-first scripts, languages, licensing, built-in AI agents, and deployment. Which to run.
- [CrewAI Setup Guide and Production Fixes (2026): Install, Observability, Alternatives](https://joshuaopolko.com/crewai-setup-production-guide/) (2026-06-13): Install CrewAI in 2026 with the exact uv and pip commands, then fix the production problems people actually hit: runaway tool-calling loops, slow crews, black-box prompts, memory at scale, and choosing between CrewAI, LangGraph, AutoGen/AG2, Agno and PydanticAI.
- [Dify Self-Hosted Guide (2026): Docker Setup, RAG, Alternatives](https://joshuaopolko.com/dify-self-hosted-guide/) (2026-06-13): Self-host Dify with Docker Compose in 2026, connect your models, build RAG and agent apps, and fix the real problems: RAM and resource limits, upgrade migrations, RAG quality, and Dify vs Flowise vs Langflow vs n8n.
- [Driftlights: WebXR Depth of Field and the Spatial Judgment Behind Architectural VR](https://joshuaopolko.com/driftlights/) (2026-06-13): A live, in-browser WebXR light field with real bokeh, parallax, and object permanence. The same true-depth cues that let an architect judge clearances, sightlines and ceiling height at 1:1 scale. Orbit it, leave your own lights, or step inside in VR.
- [n8n Self-Hosted Setup Guide (2026): Docker, AI Agents, Scaling](https://joshuaopolko.com/n8n-self-hosted-guide/) (2026-06-13): Self-host n8n with Docker in 2026, build AI agent workflows, and fix the real problems: queue-mode scaling, webhooks breaking after upgrades, backups, and how n8n compares to Make and Zapier on cost.
- [Perplexica (Now Vane) Self-Hosted Guide (2026): Open-Source Perplexity](https://joshuaopolko.com/perplexica-self-hosted-guide/) (2026-06-13): Self-host Perplexica, now rebranded Vane, in 2026: one Docker image with a bundled SearXNG, connect Ollama or a hosted model, and fix the common Ollama connection errors. Plus how it compares to Perplexity and Morphic.
- [SearXNG Self-Hosted Guide (2026): Run Your Own Private Metasearch Engine](https://joshuaopolko.com/searxng-self-hosted-guide/) (2026-06-13): Self-host SearXNG in 2026 with the exact Docker commands, plus how its metasearch design quietly makes you un-profilable: 262 upstream engines, no tracking, no ads, no fingerprint.
- [Your Site as AI Infrastructure: How to Become a Trusted Source for ChatGPT and Claude](https://joshuaopolko.com/ai-infrastructure/) (2026-06-10): AI assistants don't reward link popularity - they reward trustworthy, specific, structured data. How to build a site that becomes part of AI infrastructure, with real bot traffic data.
- [GEO: Optimizing for AI Citation Instead of Clicks](https://joshuaopolko.com/geo-ai-citation/) (2026-06-10): GEO replaces click optimization with citation optimization. Real data: ChatGPT-User bot hits ramping, Bing citations 0 to 97/day in 120 days, /answers cited within 72 hours of launch.
- [The LLM-as-ETL Architecture: Building a Data Pipeline Around Claude Instead of Bolting It On](https://joshuaopolko.com/llm-etl-architecture/) (2026-06-10): Most apps bolt LLMs onto existing pipelines. NowServingTO built the whole thing around one: Haiku classification, web_search verification, tiered self-healing re-checks, static output at $0.35/day.
- [Building an Autonomous AI Agent for Moltbook - Joshua Opolko](https://joshuaopolko.com/building-an-autonomous-ai-agent-for-moltbook/) (2026-02-04): Learn how I built an autonomous AI agent for Moltbook using JOSIEFIED-Qwen3:8b with local inference, vector memory, and secure architecture. No OpenClaw vulnerabilities, just focused AI social network automation with critical perspective.

Discover the sacred art of LED cultivation, soil microbes, and the Silicon Sol in this unique guide to hydroponic enlightenment and year-round growing.

Religious frameworks offering literal immortality serve as primary terror management, but structures vary in control and certainty provided. Death terror intensity predicts preference for authoritarian systems offering maximum control versus flexible frameworks tolerating ambiguity. - [How Intra-Community Trauma Shapes the Architecture of Belonging - Joshua Opolko](https://joshuaopolko.com/how-intra-community-trauma-shapes-the-architecture-of-belonging/) (2026-01-08): ABSTRACT Trauma inflicted by one’s primary community, be it family, ethnic group, or social tribe, creates a unique Relational Paradox. The individual develops hypersensitive survival adaptations (rapid environmental scanning, pattern recognition, and cognitive speed) specifically to navigate that community’s threats. - [Child Abuse in Religious Communities: What the Research Shows](https://joshuaopolko.com/child-abuse-in-religious-communities-what-the-research-shows/) (2026-01-04): Comprehensive research analysis of child abuse risk in religious communities. Evidence shows elevated risk in high-control groups with authoritarian structures and corporal punishment doctrines.

Repeated violence causes fascial calcification that compresses nerves, producing hypervigilance, emotional dysregulation, and baseline activation typically diagnosed as PTSD or addiction. - [AnythingLLM: Self-Hosted RAG and Chat for Local and Cloud LLMs](https://joshuaopolko.com/anythingllm-self-hosted-guide/): AnythingLLM is a free, self-hosted platform for chatting with documents and running AI agents using any LLM. Docker install, workspace setup, RAG configuration, and common issues.
- [cyubeVR: A Voxel VR World Worth Getting Lost In](https://joshuaopolko.com/cyubevr/): cyubeVR is a from-scratch voxel sandbox for PC VR, gorgeous lighting, an infinite world, and deep mod support. Why this solo-built VR game still holds a special place for me.
- [D-Lusion Rubberduck, Drumstation DT-010, and DAS: The Full Suite (Free Download)](https://joshuaopolko.com/d-lusion-rubberduck/): D-Lusion made the first TB-303 software synth for PC in 1996. Here is the history of Rubberduck, Drumstation DT-010, and the Digital Audio Server that synced them, plus clean installers for all three.
- [AEO, Answered: Real AI-Citation Results, Why Some Sites Get Cited & What Tools Should Do](https://joshuaopolko.com/geo-answers/): First-party answers to the questions people actually ask about getting cited by AI: whether AEO produces real results, why some sites get cited consistently and others don't, and what an AI-visibility tool should actually do.
- [The GEO Field Manual: 10 Steps to Get Cited by ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity & Gemini](https://joshuaopolko.com/geo-field-manual/): The complete, free playbook for getting cited by AI. Ten technical steps, the data behind each, copy-paste prompts, and a master prompt you can hand any AI. Built to be cited.
- [LibreChat: Self-Hosted ChatGPT Alternative With Multi-Provider AI Support](https://joshuaopolko.com/librechat-self-hosted-guide/): LibreChat is a free, self-hosted ChatGPT alternative connecting OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Ollama, and 10-plus other AI providers in one interface. Docker setup guide.
- [LiteLLM Proxy: One OpenAI-Compatible API for 100-Plus LLM Providers](https://joshuaopolko.com/litellm-proxy-guide/): LiteLLM Proxy gives you a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint for 100-plus LLM providers. Docker setup, config.yaml, load balancing, fallbacks, virtual keys, and troubleshooting.
- [Open WebUI: Install and Configure the Best Interface for Ollama and Local LLMs](https://joshuaopolko.com/open-webui-self-hosted-guide/): Open WebUI is the most popular self-hosted interface for Ollama and local LLMs. Docker install, Ollama connection, RAG setup, user management, and common issues.
- [XR Visual Tests](https://joshuaopolko.com/xr-tests/): WebXR moiré interference demos running in the browser. Open on a Meta Quest to go fully immersive via WebXR.

## Tools

- [AI Readiness Scanner](https://joshuaopolko.com/aiscan/): Free GEO readiness scan. Check whether ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews can read and cite your pages. Covers AI bot access, JSON-LD structured data, citability, sitemap health, and technical signals. No account or API key needed.
